summary: Extend generic_html.py to recognize CCEL content containers
description:
CCEL (ccel.org) uses <div class="book-content"> as its primary content container and <div class="bbook"> as a secondary wrapper. Neither class is in generic_html.py's content_classes selector list (line 112), so extraction falls through to soup.body.get_text() which pulls in CCEL's full navigation chrome (header, sidebar, footnote panel — none of which are tagged <nav>/<aside>).

Concrete fix in scripts/downloaders/generic_html.py line 112:

  content_classes = ["content", "main-content", "text", "body", "article-content"]

becomes:

  content_classes = ["content", "main-content", "text", "body", "article-content", "book-content", "bbook"]

Precondition: sanity-grep raw/ to confirm no existing source HTML has class='book-content' or class='bbook' that we'd start picking up unintentionally. 'bbook' is CCEL-specific enough to be safe; 'book-content' is generic enough to want verification.

Done contract: selectors added, grep shows no collisions in existing raw/, re-running acquire.py for one existing html source (e.g., a Hermeticum tractate) produces byte-identical raw output (no regression).

Rationale for editing generic_html.py rather than forking a ccel.py downloader: CCEL has no different control flow (single-page fetch, no index walking), so the fix is purely a selector-list addition. gnosis_org.py is arguably already a YAGNI artifact in the codebase; not worth repeating the pattern. If CCEL later needs pagination/footnote handling for longer works, fork to ccel.py at that point with a real reason.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Ticket spec was correct that CCEL needs book-content in the selector list, but incomplete: 'bbook' is on

tags not

, so adding it to content_classes is a no-op (find_all('div', class_='bbook') matches nothing). More importantly, CCEL also exposes
and
which are already matched by existing content_ids, AND those divs are ancestors of the book-content divs. With the current append-all logic, extraction yields ~3x duplication (id=main contains id=content contains all book-content divs, each get_text()'d separately and concatenated). Adding book-content alone helps quality but worsens duplication. Real fix: change extraction to first-tier-wins priority — semantic tags > content classes > content ids > body fallback. Drop 'bbook' from the proposed selector addition.

resolution.note: All subtasks complete. generic_html.py now extracts CCEL content cleanly. T1's Eckhart child (f1d19981) is unblocked.
